reasoning: A US President visiting Russia is extremely rare under normal circumstances, and given the ongoing war in Ukraine, such a visit would carry massive political and diplomatic consequences. Recent developments indicate that President Trump is actively engaged in negotiating a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine, having spoken to Putin for two hours on May 19. While both sides have expressed an interest in an in-person summit, discussions of the location have centered exclusively on neutral third-party sites. The Vatican was proposed but rejected by Russia, and recent reports from late May suggest Geneva as the likely venue for a trilateral meeting involving Trump, Putin, and Zelenskyy. There are no credible reports or signals indicating that Trump plans to visit Russia before July 1, 2025. Given the standard diplomatic practice of holding high-stakes peace summits in neutral locations and the lack of any evidence for a Moscow trip, the probability of this event occurring is effectively at the minimum bound.
